paper.title: Stellar Paternity Tests: Matching High-Latitude B Stars to the Open Clusters of their Birth

extraction.summary: This paper traces runaway stars ejected from open clusters but does not present any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. All discussed objects are ordinary runaway stars with ejection velocities (37-160 km/s) well below Galactic escape velocity. The paper does not claim any star is Galactic-unbound.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Ejection probabilities and characteristics of runaway star-cluster matches
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway stars ejected from open clusters (dynamical ejection or binary supernova scenarios), but does not discuss them as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. Ejection velocities (37-160 km/s) are well below typical Galactic escape velocities.

source_refs.context: Abstract describes the study as tracing runaway stars back to open clusters of birth, not as Galactic-unbound candidates
Conclusions state the paper found runaway star candidates matched to parent clusters, with no mention of Galactic-unbound status

candidate_groups_considered.description: Calculated properties of open clusters used as potential parent systems
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This table lists open cluster properties (positions, distances, densities, ages), not candidate high-velocity or Galactic-unbound stars. The clusters themselves are not presented as unbound objects.

source_refs.context: Table caption describes cluster properties, not candidate unbound stars
